problem_statement: AI-assisted 'vibe coding' generates code that prioritizes functional correctness over security, systematically introducing vulnerabilities that reactive, post-hoc oversight (code review, static analysis) cannot keep pace with as AI accelerates code volume.
magnitude_evidence: Pearce et al. found GitHub Copilot produces vulnerable code in approximately 40% of security-relevant scenarios; Liu et al. [2026] studied 31,132 AI agent skills and found 26.1% contain at least one security vulnerability
insufficiency_evidence: Traditional security practices (code reviews, penetration testing, static analysis) operate as post-hoc verification, detecting vulnerabilities after introduction rather than preventing creation; when AI accelerates code generation by orders of magnitude, reactive security processes become inadequate; Perry et al. showed developers using AI assistance write less secure code
solution_mechanisms: constitutional security constraints in spec layer
versioned machine-readable constitution with CWE mappings
